content:
# Magnesite Deposits

**Summary**: A mineral deposit containing magnesium carbonate.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991]] studied the Zarghat magnesite deposit in the Arabian Shield.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] characterized the magnesite deposits in terms of mineralogy and texture.

## Depositional Environment



- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] characterized the magnesite deposits in terms of mineralogy and texture.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991]] enrichment of Mg at the expense of Ca during diagenesis

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The deposit contains approximately 3.2 million tonnes of magnesite. ([[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991, p. 106]])

- The magnesite is mostly cryptocrystalline, massive but at places sheared and fragmented. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 22]])
